plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the single-name mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI and QQQ allocations to 25% each, favoring income and broad index exposure.
Establish a 15% cash buffer for capital preservation and stability.
This plan significantly reduces overall risk and leverage.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets approximately 1.5% per month, prioritizing capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, but will likely have lower growth potential and still carries market risk.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the single-name mandate and reduce concentration.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to reduce leveraged ETF exposure to zero.
Increase QQQI to 20% and QQQ to 20%, balancing income and broad index.
Establish a 10% cash buffer for flexibility and risk management.
Buy NVDU at 5% to add a quality tech setup from the scanner.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets approximately 2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces leverage and concentration while maintaining some growth potential, but still has moderate market exposure and associated risks.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Reduce QQQI to 10% and SMH to 5% to free capital for higher growth.
Add SOXL to 5% to leverage semiconductor upside, adhering to limits.
Buy NOW and NVDU at 10% each, leaning into high-scoring scanner setups.
Initiate a 5% position in SOXS as a potential hedge with high R.
This plan maintains a small cash buffer of 5% and uses wider, but strict, stops.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets approximately 4% per month, aiming for stretch growth. It significantly increases exposure to leveraged ETFs and high-beta names, which amplifies both upside and drawdown. Be aware of daily decay/rebalancing in leveraged ETFs and the potential for rapid, substantial losses if stops are not strictly adhered to.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Increase QQQI to 30% to prioritize income and covered-call exposure.
Reduce QQQ to 10% to shift focus towards income generation.
Sell SMH and CRWD to eliminate high-beta single-name exposure.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to cut all leveraged ETFs to zero.
Maintain a 15% cash buffer for stability and liquidity.
income.read: This income plan targets approximately 1% per month, focusing on parking cash for yield. It prioritizes income and low drawdown, but distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in a selloff. This is not a savings account and still carries market risk.
